mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0 percent, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable monetary policy confirms robust consumer spending power, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or positive return frequency drops below 45 percent when both series trend upward concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening supply-side capacity and business investment, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during recovery phases, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 when INDPRO trends upward.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect economic resilience and rising household income, supporting corporate earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive return probability drops below 50 percent during payroll expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 305.0, persistent inflation forces aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of high-inflation observations, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy with Rank IC below 0.02 during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in housing starts and low unemployment confirms synchronized demand across construction and consumer sectors, boosting equity valuations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over univariate models, or positive return frequency falls below 48 percent when both conditions align.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals broad-based economic expansion, increasing corporate profit forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict positive returns during early-cycle phases, or 60-day forward returns show negative skew when GDPC1 growth accelerates.
